In just the last one year, health insurance claims worth ₹26,000 crore were rejected in India.
That means 1 out of every 10 health insurance claims gets rejected.

People keep paying premiums for years…
But when the time comes to file a claim, the insurance company says —
“Claim denied.”

In this video, I explain why health insurance claims get rejected and share real-life cases where people had to pay hospital bills from their own pocket — despite having insurance worth lakhs and even crores.

If you already have health insurance, or you’re planning to buy one,
👉 watch this video till the end — missing even one point can cost you lakhs of rupees

comments.text:
A small addition: Just checking Claim Settlement Ratio (CSR) is not enough. It doesn't account for the amount settled. An insurance company might partially settle many claims (paying less than the full amount) and still show a high CSR. 

Also check Incurred Claim Ratio (ICR). This measures the proportion of premiums collected that an insurance company pays out as claims. For instance, if an insurer collects ₹100 crore in premiums and pays out ₹85 crore in claims, the ICR is 85%.

A high ICR indicates the insurance company is paying out a lot of what it is earning from premiums. So while CSR is a trustworthy signal for claim reliability, it's wise to look at both ratios together. Both these metrics are published annually by by IRDAI (Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India).
